title: Hadwiger's Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
statement_text: If $G$ is a graph which does not contain $K_t$ as a minor, then $\chi(G) \leq t-1$.
context_text: Hadwiger's conjecture, raised in 1943, is described as one of the most central open problems in graph theory. The best known asymptotic upper bound for a long time was $O(t\sqrt{\log t})$; recently Delcourt and Postle proved an $O(t\log\log t)$ upper bound. The paper's main result shows that any $f(t)$ coloring bound for $K_t$-minor-free graphs yields a $2f(t)$ bound for odd $K_t$-minor-free graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves that any upper bound $f(t)$ on the chromatic number of $K_t$-minor-free graphs implies a $2f(t)$ upper bound for odd $K_t$-minor-free graphs, establishing asymptotic equivalence between the two conjectures up to a factor of 2.
paper_title: Asymptotic Equivalence of Hadwiger's Conjecture and its Odd Minor-Variant

title: Odd Hadwiger's Conjecture (Conjecture 2)
statement_text: If $G$ is a graph which does not contain $K_t$ as an odd minor, then $\chi(G) \leq t-1$.
context_text: Conjectured by Gerards and Seymour as a qualitative strengthening of Hadwiger's conjecture. The asymptotic relaxation — whether graphs with no odd $K_t$-minor are $O(t)$-colorable — remains open. Prior to this paper, the best known bound was $O(t(\log\log t)^2)$ by Delcourt and Postle.
paper_contribution: Improves the asymptotic upper bound on the chromatic number of odd $K_t$-minor-free graphs from $O(t(\log\log t)^2)$ to $O(t\log\log t)$, by proving that such graphs are $2f(t)$-colorable whenever $K_t$-minor-free graphs are $f(t)$-colorable, then applying the Delcourt–Postle $O(t\log\log t)$ bound for Hadwiger.
paper_title: Asymptotic Equivalence of Hadwiger's Conjecture and its Odd Minor-Variant
